name: The Fool
keywords_upright: new beginnings; spontaneity; innocence; leap of faith; free spirit
keywords_reversed: recklessness; hesitation; naivety; poor judgment; fear of change
meaning_upright: The Fool signals a fresh start taken with an open heart, stepping into the unknown with trust rather than a detailed plan. It points to spontaneity, beginner's mind, and the willingness to risk looking foolish in order to grow.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, The Fool suggests either a reckless leap taken without thought or a journey postponed out of fear. It points to hesitation, naivety about real risks, or holding back from a beginning that is ready to happen.
love: In love, The Fool suggests a fresh romantic chapter, playful energy, and following the heart without guarantees.
career: In career, it points to a new venture, role, or idea that rewards courage and an open mind over rigid planning.
mood: The mood is light, curious, and playful — an openness that welcomes surprises rather than bracing against them.
spiritual: Spiritually, The Fool invites beginner's mind: trusting the journey before the map exists and treating the unknown as sacred ground.

name: The Magician
keywords_upright: manifestation; willpower; resourcefulness; skill; focused action
keywords_reversed: manipulation; untapped talent; scattered energy; trickery; illusion
meaning_upright: The Magician shows that every tool needed is already on the table; it is a card of manifestation through focused will and skill. It suggests aligning intention, words, and action to turn an idea into reality.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, The Magician warns of scattered focus, wasted talent, or someone using charm and cleverness to manipulate. It can point to promises that sound impressive but lack substance.
love: In love, The Magician suggests attraction built on confidence and communication, and the power to actively create the relationship you want.
career: In career, it signals skill meeting opportunity — a strong moment to pitch, launch, or negotiate.
mood: The mood is confident and energized — a focused spark that makes things feel possible right now.
spiritual: Spiritually, The Magician is about alignment: bringing thought, word, and action into one channel so intention becomes creation.

name: The High Priestess
keywords_upright: intuition; inner wisdom; mystery; the subconscious; stillness
keywords_reversed: ignored intuition; secrets; disconnection from self; hidden agendas; surface knowledge
meaning_upright: The High Priestess asks for stillness and listening; the answer is already known at an intuitive level even if it cannot yet be explained. She represents hidden knowledge, the subconscious, and truths that reveal themselves in their own time.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, she suggests intuition being drowned out by noise, other people's opinions, or a refusal to look inward. Secrets may be at play, or an inner knowing is being ignored because it is inconvenient.
love: In love, The High Priestess points to unspoken feelings and undercurrents — trust what is sensed rather than only what is said.
career: In career, it advises watching and listening before acting; information is still hidden and patience reveals it.
mood: The mood is quiet, inward, and softly mysterious — a day for listening more than speaking.
spiritual: Spiritually, she points to the inner voice: intuition, dreams, and knowledge that arrives in stillness rather than study.

name: The Empress
keywords_upright: abundance; nurturing; fertility; creativity; sensuality
keywords_reversed: creative block; smothering; self-neglect; dependence; stagnation
meaning_upright: The Empress embodies abundance, nurture, and creative fertility — things growing because they are cared for. She encourages receiving pleasure, tending relationships and projects patiently, and trusting natural growth.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, The Empress points to creative block, self-neglect, or care that has tipped into smothering or dependence. Growth stalls because the ground itself — body, home, heart — is being ignored.
love: In love, The Empress is one of the warmest cards, indicating deepening affection, nurturing partnership, and sensual connection.
career: In career, it favors creative projects and steady cultivation — what is tended now will bear fruit.
mood: The mood is warm, sensual, and content — comfort in the body and pleasure in simple things.
spiritual: Spiritually, The Empress teaches abundance: creation flows through care, and tending something gently is itself a devotion.

name: The Emperor
keywords_upright: structure; authority; stability; leadership; discipline
keywords_reversed: rigidity; control issues; domination; lack of discipline; abuse of power
meaning_upright: The Emperor stands for structure, boundaries, and earned authority — building something stable through discipline and clear rules. He suggests taking charge, acting decisively, and protecting what has been built.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, The Emperor warns of control hardening into rigidity or domination, or conversely a lack of structure that lets things drift. Power struggles and inflexibility may be blocking progress.
love: In love, The Emperor points to a stabilizing, protective presence, though it cautions against controlling behavior replacing warmth.
career: In career, it signals leadership, order, and long-term building — take responsibility and set the framework.
mood: The mood is steady, resolute, and composed — feet planted, emotions in order.
spiritual: Spiritually, The Emperor frames discipline as devotion: structure and healthy boundaries as ways of honoring what matters.

name: The Hierophant
keywords_upright: tradition; guidance; shared values; institutions; mentorship
keywords_reversed: rebellion; questioning convention; restriction; dogma; breaking rules
meaning_upright: The Hierophant represents tradition, teachers, and the wisdom of established paths — learning from those who walked the road before. It suggests seeking guidance, honoring commitments, and finding meaning in shared values or ritual.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, The Hierophant points to chafing against convention — rules, institutions, or expectations that no longer fit. It can signal the need to think for oneself, or a warning against dogma on either side.
love: In love, The Hierophant often relates to commitment and shared values, sometimes literally marriage or formalizing a bond.
career: In career, it favors established institutions, mentorship, and proven methods over improvisation.
mood: The mood is reflective and respectful — drawn toward counsel, study, or familiar ritual.
spiritual: Spiritually, The Hierophant honors lineage: wisdom passed down through teachers, tradition, and shared practice.

name: The Lovers
keywords_upright: love; union; alignment; meaningful choice; harmony
keywords_reversed: disharmony; misalignment; avoidance of choice; temptation; imbalance
meaning_upright: The Lovers speaks of deep connection and alignment of hearts and values, and also of a significant choice made consciously. It asks that the decision reflect who you truly are, not merely what is convenient.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, The Lovers points to disharmony, mismatched values, or a choice being avoided. Something is out of alignment between what is felt, what is said, and what is done.
love: In love, this is the card of genuine union, mutual attraction, and a relationship built on real compatibility.
career: In career, it points to a values-based crossroads or a partnership that must be chosen deliberately.
mood: The mood is tender and heart-open — connection feels close and choices feel meaningful.
spiritual: Spiritually, The Lovers is union and alignment: choosing from the soul so that love and values point the same way.

name: The Chariot
keywords_upright: determination; victory; willpower; self-control; momentum
keywords_reversed: lack of direction; aggression; obstacles; scattered forces; loss of control
meaning_upright: The Chariot is a card of victory through will — holding opposing forces together and driving them in one chosen direction. It signals momentum, discipline, and success for those who keep their hands firmly on the reins.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, The Chariot suggests energy pulling in conflicting directions, forcing an outcome, or momentum stalling. The path forward needs steering, not more speed.
love: In love, The Chariot suggests pursuing the relationship with intention and overcoming distance or obstacles together.
career: In career, it signals ambition rewarded — drive, focus, and control carry a project across the line.
mood: The mood is driven and determined — momentum with hands firmly on the reins.
spiritual: Spiritually, The Chariot is mastery of opposites: holding conflicting forces together and steering them by will toward one aim.

name: Strength
keywords_upright: courage; inner strength; patience; compassion; gentle power
keywords_reversed: self-doubt; raw emotion; impatience; insecurity; forcing
meaning_upright: Strength shows power expressed as patience and compassion — taming the lion with a steady hand rather than a whip. It points to quiet courage, resilience, and mastering impulses instead of being ruled by them.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, Strength reveals self-doubt, depleted courage, or emotions running the show. It asks for self-compassion first; the inner lion is frightened, not gone.
love: In love, Strength speaks of patience, emotional honesty, and a bond that grows through gentleness rather than pressure.
career: In career, it favors persistence and calm confidence over aggressive tactics — influence beats force.
mood: The mood is calm courage — soft on the outside, unshakeable underneath.
spiritual: Spiritually, Strength teaches that gentleness is power: the inner beast is tamed by compassion, not force.

name: The Hermit
keywords_upright: introspection; solitude; inner guidance; wisdom; searching
keywords_reversed: isolation; loneliness; withdrawal; lost direction; avoidance
meaning_upright: The Hermit withdraws from noise to find the answer by his own lamp — a card of introspection, soul-searching, and hard-won wisdom. It suggests the next step becomes clear in solitude, not in the crowd.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, The Hermit warns that healthy solitude has slipped into isolation, or that reflection is being used to avoid life. It can also mean refusing guidance that is genuinely needed.
love: In love, The Hermit often signals a period of reflection about what one truly needs, or a partner who requires space.
career: In career, it favors research, deep work, and stepping back to reassess the path before advancing.
mood: The mood is solitary and contemplative — a pull toward quiet and one's own company.
spiritual: Spiritually, The Hermit is the inner lamp: withdrawing from noise so the soul's own light can show the next step.

name: Wheel of Fortune
keywords_upright: change; cycles; destiny; turning point; luck
keywords_reversed: resistance to change; bad luck; repeating cycles; setbacks; clinging
meaning_upright: The Wheel of Fortune marks a turning point: circumstances are shifting, often through forces beyond personal control. It counsels riding the change with flexibility, since what rises and falls is part of a larger cycle.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the Wheel points to resisting a change that is happening anyway, or a cycle repeating because its lesson has not been learned. Delays and reversals ease once the pattern is acknowledged.
love: In love, the Wheel signals a destined shift — chance meetings, changing dynamics, or a relationship entering a new phase.
career: In career, it points to changing circumstances and timely opportunities; stay adaptable and catch the upswing.
mood: The mood is anticipatory and changeable — the sense that something is turning.
spiritual: Spiritually, the Wheel teaches cycles: seasons rise and fall by design, and trust means moving with the turn rather than against it.

name: Justice
keywords_upright: fairness; truth; accountability; cause and effect; clarity
keywords_reversed: unfairness; dishonesty; avoiding accountability; bias; imbalance
meaning_upright: Justice deals in truth, fairness, and consequence: decisions and actions are being weighed, and outcomes will match what was earned. It calls for honesty, balanced judgment, and taking responsibility for one's part.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, Justice points to unfairness, self-deception, or dodging accountability. Something is out of balance, and restoring it requires facing an uncomfortable truth.
love: In love, Justice asks for honesty and equal give-and-take; imbalances in effort or truth come due.
career: In career, it relates to contracts, negotiations, and fair dealing — integrity now determines the outcome.
mood: The mood is sober and clear-eyed — weighing things honestly, including oneself.
spiritual: Spiritually, Justice is karma and truth: actions ripple back, and integrity is its own form of alignment.

name: The Hanged Man
keywords_upright: surrender; new perspective; pause; letting go; suspension
keywords_reversed: stalling; resistance; martyrdom; indecision; wasted sacrifice
meaning_upright: The Hanged Man asks for willing pause and a change of perspective — progress right now comes from surrender, not pushing. Seen upside down, the problem often reveals a solution effort could never force.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, The Hanged Man shows a pause that has become stalling, or sacrifice without purpose. It suggests resisting a needed letting-go, or playing the martyr instead of making a decision.
love: In love, The Hanged Man counsels patience and seeing the other person's viewpoint before acting.
career: In career, it suggests putting a plan on hold and re-examining assumptions — the delay itself is informative.
mood: The mood is suspended and strangely peaceful — waiting that asks not to be rushed.
spiritual: Spiritually, The Hanged Man is surrender: releasing control long enough for a completely new perspective to arrive.

name: Death
keywords_upright: endings; transformation; transition; release; renewal
keywords_reversed: resistance to endings; stagnation; fear of change; holding on; incomplete closure
meaning_upright: Death marks a definitive ending that clears space for transformation — a chapter closing so a truer one can begin. It rarely concerns literal death; it is the card of necessary release and renewal.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, Death shows an ending being resisted: clinging to what is already over and prolonging the discomfort of transition. Stagnation persists until the release is allowed.
love: In love, Death signals the end of a dynamic or relationship phase, making room for a more honest connection.
career: In career, it points to closing a role, project, or identity — transformation follows the letting-go.
mood: The mood is heavy yet quietly relieved — the weight of an ending already loosening its grip.
spiritual: Spiritually, Death is transformation: every rebirth begins with a release, and what falls away makes room for what is truly next.

name: Temperance
keywords_upright: balance; moderation; patience; blending; healing
keywords_reversed: excess; imbalance; impatience; friction; overcorrection
meaning_upright: Temperance blends opposites into harmony — a card of moderation, patience, and finding the middle path. It suggests healing through steady adjustment rather than dramatic moves.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, Temperance reveals excess or imbalance: too much of one thing, too little of another, and friction as a result. It calls for recalibration before the imbalance causes real damage.
love: In love, Temperance indicates growing harmony, patience with differences, and a relationship finding its natural rhythm.
career: In career, it favors steady progress, collaboration, and balancing competing demands without burning out.
mood: The mood is serene and even — nothing extreme, everything gently blended.
spiritual: Spiritually, Temperance is alchemy: mixing opposites with patience until they become something finer than either alone.

name: The Devil
keywords_upright: attachment; temptation; unhealthy patterns; materialism; feeling trapped
keywords_reversed: breaking free; reclaiming power; awareness; release; recovery
meaning_upright: The Devil exposes chains that are worn voluntarily — addictions, obsessions, toxic dynamics, or fears that keep one bound. It asks an honest look at what feels impossible to leave but is actually a choice.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, The Devil is the moment of breaking free: seeing the pattern clearly and reclaiming power from it. Old attachments loosen once they are named.
love: In love, The Devil can indicate intense attraction shadowed by possessiveness, dependency, or patterns that diminish both people.
career: In career, it warns of golden handcuffs, burnout loops, or compromising values for material security.
mood: The mood is tense and restless — tempted, tethered, or circling an old habit.
spiritual: Spiritually, The Devil calls for shadow work: facing the attachments that bind and remembering the chains are loose.

name: The Tower
keywords_upright: sudden upheaval; revelation; collapse of the false; disruption; awakening
keywords_reversed: averted disaster; fear of change; delayed collapse; resisting the inevitable; internal upheaval
meaning_upright: The Tower is sudden upheaval that demolishes what was built on shaky ground — shocking, but ultimately clarifying. What falls was not stable; the lightning also illuminates.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, The Tower suggests upheaval resisted, delayed, or happening internally — a quiet crisis of belief rather than an outer collapse. Propping up the unstable only postpones the rebuild.
love: In love, The Tower points to a sudden revelation or shake-up that changes the relationship's foundation and demands honesty.
career: In career, it signals abrupt disruption — restructures, failures, or truths surfacing — that clears the way for something sounder.
mood: The mood is shaken and electric — the ground moving underfoot.
spiritual: Spiritually, The Tower is awakening by lightning: false structures fall so that truth has somewhere to stand.

name: The Star
keywords_upright: hope; renewal; healing; inspiration; faith
keywords_reversed: discouragement; lost faith; disconnection; self-doubt; dimmed hope
meaning_upright: The Star is the calm after the storm — hope, healing, and quiet faith that the path ahead is good. It encourages openness, authenticity, and trusting that renewal is already underway.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, The Star shows hope running low and inspiration feeling out of reach. The light has not gone out; reconnecting to what genuinely nourishes will reveal it again.
love: In love, The Star brings healing after difficulty and renewed faith in connection — a gentle, promising sign.
career: In career, it signals renewed purpose and long-term promise; keep faith in the direction even if progress is quiet.
mood: The mood is hopeful and soothed — a quiet exhale after a long storm.
spiritual: Spiritually, The Star is grace: healing, renewal, and the faith that the universe is gently guiding, not testing.

name: The Moon
keywords_upright: illusion; intuition; uncertainty; the subconscious; dreams
keywords_reversed: clarity emerging; released fear; truth surfacing; confusion lifting; self-deception ending
meaning_upright: The Moon casts a landscape of shadows where fear and imagination distort what is real — things are not as they appear. It counsels moving slowly, trusting intuition over anxiety, and waiting for daylight before final judgments.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, The Moon marks confusion beginning to lift: secrets surface, fears shrink to their true size, and the road becomes visible again. Clarity is returning after a disorienting stretch.
love: In love, The Moon points to uncertainty, mixed signals, or projections — feelings are real but the picture is incomplete.
career: In career, it warns of unclear information or hidden factors; verify facts before committing.
mood: The mood is dreamy and uneasy — feelings vivid, facts blurry.
spiritual: Spiritually, The Moon is the subconscious speaking: dreams, symbols, and intuition lighting a path the rational mind cannot.

name: The Sun
keywords_upright: joy; success; vitality; clarity; confidence
keywords_reversed: dimmed joy; temporary setbacks; pessimism; burnout; delayed success
meaning_upright: The Sun is the deck's most unambiguously positive card: warmth, vitality, success, and truth in full daylight. It signals things genuinely going well and invites wholehearted enjoyment of it.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, The Sun does not turn dark — it dims: joy muted by pessimism, fatigue, or clouds of doubt over real success. The good is still there; perspective needs cleaning, not the circumstances.
love: In love, The Sun radiates happiness, openness, and celebration — a relationship thriving in honest light.
career: In career, it signals visible success, recognition, and energy — momentum is real and deserved.
mood: The mood is joyful and radiant — warmth, play, and easy confidence.
spiritual: Spiritually, The Sun is vitality and gratitude: clarity so bright that simply being alive feels like the blessing it is.

name: Judgement
keywords_upright: awakening; reckoning; renewal; calling; self-evaluation
keywords_reversed: self-criticism; ignoring the call; stuck in the past; harsh judgment; avoidance
meaning_upright: Judgement is the wake-up call: an honest reckoning with the past that frees one to rise into a new phase. It often marks a moment of clarity about purpose — a summons that should be answered.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, Judgement shows the call being ignored, or self-judgment so harsh it prevents moving forward. The past is reviewed on a loop instead of being released.
love: In love, Judgement brings honest evaluation of the relationship's history and a chance at genuine renewal or closure.
career: In career, it signals a career reckoning or calling — an invitation to step into work that actually matters to you.
mood: The mood is stirred and resolved — an honest reckoning that ends in relief.
spiritual: Spiritually, Judgement is the awakening call: rising from the past, forgiven and lighter, toward a truer purpose.

name: The World
keywords_upright: completion; fulfillment; wholeness; achievement; integration
keywords_reversed: incompletion; loose ends; delayed closure; shortcuts; unfinished business
meaning_upright: The World is completion: a long cycle fulfilled, lessons integrated, and a well-earned sense of wholeness. It celebrates achievement and signals readiness for the next, larger cycle.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, The World points to a goal almost reached but not closed — loose ends, missing steps, or closure being postponed. Finish the last mile; the reward waits there.
love: In love, The World signifies a relationship reaching fulfillment and harmony, or closure that finally allows a new beginning.
career: In career, it marks the successful completion of a major goal and often expansion — travel, recognition, or a bigger stage.
mood: The mood is fulfilled and celebratory — a deep sense of arrival.
spiritual: Spiritually, The World is integration: the lessons of a whole cycle woven into wholeness before the next dance begins.

name: Ace of Wands
keywords_upright: inspiration; new opportunity; creative spark; enthusiasm; potential
keywords_reversed: delays; false start; lack of motivation; blocked creativity; hesitation
meaning_upright: The Ace of Wands is a spark of pure creative potential — a new idea, passion, or opportunity bursting with energy. It invites saying yes and acting while the fire is hot.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the Ace shows inspiration that stalls: false starts, delays, or enthusiasm that fizzles before it becomes action. The spark needs fuel and commitment to catch.
love: In love, it signals a spark of fresh attraction or renewed passion in an existing bond.
career: In career, it marks the ignition of a new project or venture — promising, if acted on quickly.
mood: The mood is excited and inspired — new energy crackling to be used.
spiritual: Spiritually, the Ace of Wands is the creative spark as gift: inspiration arriving from beyond and asking to be acted on.

name: Two of Wands
keywords_upright: planning; future vision; decision; expansion; the world in hand
keywords_reversed: fear of the unknown; playing it safe; poor planning; restlessness; indecision
meaning_upright: The Two of Wands is the planner's card: standing at a threshold, world in hand, weighing how far to venture. It suggests bold vision paired with deliberate first steps.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it shows ambition trapped by fear of the unknown — staying in familiar territory while opportunity waits outside. Plans exist but the commitment to leave the castle does not.
love: In love, it points to weighing a decision about the relationship's future direction.
career: In career, it favors strategic planning and considering expansion beyond the current comfort zone.
mood: The mood is restless anticipation — plans forming, horizons calling.
spiritual: Spiritually, it expands the vision: the soul outgrowing familiar walls and daring to imagine a larger life.

name: Three of Wands
keywords_upright: progress; expansion; foresight; ships coming in; momentum
keywords_reversed: delays; obstacles to plans; limited vision; setbacks; impatience
meaning_upright: The Three of Wands shows plans in motion and horizons expanding — the ships are launched and beginning to return. It encourages confidence in the direction while staying alert for arriving opportunities.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it signals delays and shipments that haven't come in: plans meeting friction or vision proving too narrow. Reassess logistics rather than abandoning the voyage.
love: In love, it suggests a relationship moving forward, sometimes across distance, with patience being rewarded.
career: In career, it marks early results confirming a good strategy — think bigger and prepare for growth.
mood: The mood is optimistic and forward-looking — effort already sailing back as results.
spiritual: Spiritually, it teaches faithful patience: what was set in motion is growing even while it is out of sight.

name: Four of Wands
keywords_upright: celebration; homecoming; milestone; community; stability
keywords_reversed: instability at home; postponed celebration; transition; lack of support; shaky foundations
meaning_upright: The Four of Wands is celebration and homecoming — a milestone reached and shared with the people who matter. It marks stability worth pausing to enjoy before the next stage.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it points to tension at home, a celebration delayed, or a sense of not quite belonging. The foundation wants attention before the party can happen.
love: In love, it is a card of happy milestones — moving in together, engagements, or simply a joyful, secure phase.
career: In career, it marks a completed stage worth celebrating and a supportive, stable team environment.
mood: The mood is celebratory and secure — belonging, milestones, open doors.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is gratitude in community: pausing to honor how far things have come, together.

name: Five of Wands
keywords_upright: conflict; competition; friction; clashing egos; scattered effort
keywords_reversed: avoiding conflict; resolution; inner conflict; release of tension; picking battles
meaning_upright: The Five of Wands is friction: competing voices, clashing egos, and energy scattered in a scramble. The conflict is usually more noise than damage, but nothing advances until efforts align.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it shows conflict either being dodged or finally winding down. It can also point inward — competing desires fighting inside one person.
love: In love, it signals bickering, rivalry, or outside interference creating unnecessary tension.
career: In career, it points to competition and disagreement over direction — channel it into healthy rivalry, not chaos.
mood: The mood is irritable and competitive — energy scattering into friction.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is ego work: noticing the need to win and redirecting that fire into growth instead of noise.

name: Six of Wands
keywords_upright: victory; recognition; success; confidence; public acclaim
keywords_reversed: private victory; fall from grace; self-doubt; lack of recognition; ego inflation
meaning_upright: The Six of Wands is the victory parade: success achieved and publicly recognized. It confirms that effort has paid off and confidence is warranted.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it suggests success unacknowledged, confidence wobbling, or acclaim that went to the head. The win may be real but the applause is missing — or hollow.
love: In love, it signals pride in the relationship and mutual admiration, being 'seen' by a partner.
career: In career, it points to recognition, promotion, or a win that raises your profile.
mood: The mood is proud and uplifted — effort seen and cheered.
spiritual: Spiritually, it pairs success with humility: receiving recognition gracefully and honoring those who helped.

name: Seven of Wands
keywords_upright: defense; standing your ground; perseverance; conviction; holding advantage
keywords_reversed: overwhelm; giving up ground; exhaustion; defensiveness; yielding
meaning_upright: The Seven of Wands is the defense of hard-won ground: challengers press from below, but the higher position holds. It calls for conviction and stamina — the position is defensible if it's defended.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it shows defenses wearing thin: overwhelm, exhaustion, or surrendering ground that could have been held. It may also warn against defensiveness where none is needed.
love: In love, it can mean protecting the relationship from outside pressure, or defending personal boundaries within it.
career: In career, it points to defending your position, ideas, or territory against competition — persistence wins.
mood: The mood is defensive but resolute — tested, yet standing firm.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is conviction: defending what one truly believes, calmly, without needing everyone's agreement.

name: Eight of Wands
keywords_upright: swift movement; momentum; news arriving; alignment; rapid progress
keywords_reversed: delays; frustration; scattered energy; missed timing; slowdown
meaning_upright: The Eight of Wands is speed: events accelerating, messages arriving, and obstacles clearing all at once. It advises readiness — things long awaited now move quickly.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it marks delays and misfires: momentum stalling, messages crossed, timing off. Rushing against the slowdown makes it worse; re-aim before re-launching.
love: In love, it signals fast-moving romance, important messages, or a relationship suddenly gaining momentum.
career: In career, it points to rapid developments, quick decisions, and news that sets things in motion.
mood: The mood is swift and eager — messages, movement, momentum.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is flow: signs arriving quickly and the invitation to move with them rather than overthink.

name: Nine of Wands
keywords_upright: resilience; last stand; persistence; guardedness; nearly there
keywords_reversed: burnout; paranoia; giving up at the end; rigid defenses; battle fatigue
meaning_upright: The Nine of Wands is the wounded sentry still standing: battered by past battles but not beaten, with the finish line close. It counsels one more push — resilience now completes what persistence started.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it shows defenses built from old wounds doing more harm than protection, or collapse just short of the goal. Rest and reinforcement, not surrender, are what's needed.
love: In love, it points to guardedness from past hurt — the walls that protected once may now be keeping love out.
career: In career, it signals fatigue near the end of a long effort; endurance through this last stretch pays off.
mood: The mood is weary but determined — bruised, guarded, unbeaten.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is resilience as practice: tending wounds without letting them close the heart before the last step.

name: Ten of Wands
keywords_upright: burden; overload; responsibility; carrying too much; final effort
keywords_reversed: release; delegation; putting burdens down; collapse; learning to say no
meaning_upright: The Ten of Wands is success turned heavy: every wand carried alone, vision blocked by the load itself. The task is nearly done, but the card asks what can be delegated or dropped.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it is the moment of setting burdens down — delegating, quitting what isn't yours to carry, or collapsing under a load ignored too long. Release is overdue.
love: In love, it warns of one partner shouldering the relationship's weight alone — rebalancing is needed.
career: In career, it signals overload and burnout risk; delegate and prioritize before the load drops you.
mood: The mood is burdened and overloaded — carrying more than one's share.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is release: learning that putting down what was never yours to carry is also an act of faith.

name: Page of Wands
keywords_upright: enthusiasm; exploration; free spirit; exciting news; curiosity
keywords_reversed: scattered energy; hasty starts; boredom; unreliable news; restlessness
meaning_upright: The Page of Wands is the eager explorer: curious, spontaneous, and carrying news of exciting possibilities. It encourages a playful, experimental approach to a new passion.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the Page starts everything and finishes nothing — enthusiasm without follow-through, or news that disappoints. Direction, not more sparks, is what's missing.
love: In love, it brings flirtation, playful energy, or an admirer with an adventurous spirit.
career: In career, it signals a new idea or opportunity worth exploring with fresh eyes and low stakes.
mood: The mood is curious and enthusiastic — sparks everywhere, ready to explore.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is following inspiration: treating every spark of interest as a possible message from the soul.

name: Knight of Wands
keywords_upright: action; adventure; passion; bold pursuit; charisma
keywords_reversed: impulsiveness; recklessness; scattered passion; delays in action; hot temper
meaning_upright: The Knight of Wands charges in with passion and flair — a card of bold, energetic pursuit of what excites. It favors decisive action, though the follow-through matters as much as the charge.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the Knight is all gallop and no direction: impulsive moves, burned bridges, or passion that flames out. Slow the horse enough to aim it.
love: In love, it brings intense, fast-burning attraction or a charismatic pursuer — thrilling, but test the staying power.
career: In career, it signals bold moves and fast opportunities; act decisively but don't skip the details.
mood: The mood is adventurous and impulsive — hot energy that wants to move now.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is passion as fuel: honoring the fire while learning to ground it so it warms rather than burns.

name: Queen of Wands
keywords_upright: confidence; warmth; determination; magnetism; independence
keywords_reversed: insecurity; jealousy; demanding nature; burnout; dimmed confidence
meaning_upright: The Queen of Wands is warm, confident, and magnetic — someone who owns a room without demanding it. She encourages leading with authenticity, courage, and generous fire.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the Queen's fire turns inward or sour: insecurity behind bravado, jealousy, or warmth depleted by overextension. Her confidence returns when she stops performing it.
love: In love, she signals passionate, self-assured energy — attraction rooted in independence rather than need.
career: In career, she embodies confident leadership and creative drive that inspires others.
mood: The mood is confident and magnetic — warmth that fills a room.
spiritual: Spiritually, she is authentic radiance: self-expression as a form of generosity, shining without apology.

name: King of Wands
keywords_upright: visionary leadership; boldness; entrepreneurship; mastery of fire; inspiration
keywords_reversed: arrogance; tyranny; impulsive leadership; unfulfilled vision; domineering
meaning_upright: The King of Wands is the visionary leader: fire mastered and directed at long-range goals. He inspires others to build his vision and rewards boldness backed by experience.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the King becomes domineering or erratic — vision without patience, authority without listening. Leadership recovers when ego steps aside for the mission.
love: In love, he represents a confident, protective partner with big plans — generous, though he must share the wheel.
career: In career, he signals entrepreneurial vision and decisive leadership — think big and commit fully.
mood: The mood is bold and visionary — leadership energy, eyes on the horizon.
spiritual: Spiritually, he is purpose embodied: directing creative fire toward a vision larger than the self.

name: Ace of Cups
keywords_upright: new love; emotional beginning; compassion; overflowing heart; spiritual opening
keywords_reversed: blocked emotions; emptiness; repressed feelings; emotional loss; self-love needed
meaning_upright: The Ace of Cups is the heart overflowing: new love, deep compassion, or an emotional and spiritual awakening. It invites receiving and giving feeling freely, without rationing.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the Ace shows the cup tipped or blocked — emotions withheld, love unexpressed, or an inner emptiness asking for attention. Refilling starts with self-compassion.
love: In love, it is one of the strongest cards for new romance or a fresh emotional depth in an existing bond.
career: In career, it signals emotionally fulfilling work, creative flow, or warm new connections.
mood: The mood is tender and open-hearted — feelings close to the surface, in the best way.
spiritual: Spiritually, the Ace of Cups is love as opening: the heart offered and refilled from a source deeper than circumstance.

name: Two of Cups
keywords_upright: partnership; mutual attraction; connection; harmony; union of equals
keywords_reversed: imbalance; broken connection; tension; miscommunication; self-partnership needed
meaning_upright: The Two of Cups is mutual recognition: two people meeting as equals in attraction, friendship, or alliance. It marks a genuine bond where feeling flows both ways.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it shows a connection out of balance — one cup fuller than the other, trust strained, or harmony disrupted. Repair begins with honest, equal exchange.
love: In love, it is the card of mutual attraction and partnership — a bond of genuine reciprocity.
career: In career, it favors partnerships, collaborations, and one-on-one alliances built on mutual respect.
mood: The mood is affectionate and harmonious — met, mirrored, understood.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is sacred connection: another person as a mirror in which the soul recognizes itself.

name: Three of Cups
keywords_upright: celebration; friendship; community; joy shared; creative collaboration
keywords_reversed: overindulgence; third-party interference; isolation from friends; gossip; excess
meaning_upright: The Three of Cups is friendship and shared joy — the toast among people who genuinely celebrate each other. It encourages leaning into community and marking wins together.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it warns of overindulgence, gossip, or a third presence straining a bond. It can also signal withdrawal from friends when their support is most needed.
love: In love, it brings celebration and social warmth, though reversed it can flag outside interference in the relationship.
career: In career, it signals successful teamwork and collaborative wins worth celebrating.
mood: The mood is joyful and sociable — laughter shared, cups raised.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is communion: gratitude multiplied by being celebrated among kindred spirits.

name: Four of Cups
keywords_upright: apathy; contemplation; disconnection; missed offer; reevaluation
keywords_reversed: renewed interest; accepting the offer; emerging from withdrawal; new motivation; openness
meaning_upright: The Four of Cups is emotional weariness: arms crossed, absorbed in discontent, while a new cup is offered unseen. It asks whether the boredom is real or whether attention has simply turned away from what's available.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it marks the end of apathy — noticing the offered cup at last and re-engaging with life. Motivation returns as attention comes back to the present.
love: In love, it suggests emotional distance or taking a partner for granted; an offer of connection may be going unnoticed.
career: In career, it signals disengagement or an overlooked opportunity — look again before saying nothing interests you.
mood: The mood is apathetic and withdrawn — full cups nearby, appetite gone.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is a call to presence: noticing the blessing being offered before deciding nothing is there.

name: Five of Cups
keywords_upright: loss; grief; regret; focusing on what's gone; disappointment
keywords_reversed: acceptance; moving on; forgiveness; seeing what remains; healing
meaning_upright: The Five of Cups is grief fixed on the three spilled cups while two still stand full behind. The loss is real and deserves mourning — and the card gently notes that not everything is lost.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it marks the turn toward healing: accepting what happened, forgiving, and finally seeing the cups still standing. Recovery has begun.
love: In love, it points to grieving a loss or disappointment — and to the remaining connection that grief may be obscuring.
career: In career, it signals a setback worth mourning briefly, then salvaging — something valuable survived the spill.
mood: The mood is sorrowful and regretful — attention fixed on what spilled.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is grief as teacher: mourning honestly, then turning to the cups that still stand.

name: Six of Cups
keywords_upright: nostalgia; childhood; innocence; reunion; simple kindness
keywords_reversed: stuck in the past; rose-tinted memory; leaving home; growing up; releasing old ties
meaning_upright: The Six of Cups is sweet memory and simple kindness — nostalgia, reunions, and the healing comfort of the familiar. It can herald someone from the past returning or old joys revisited.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it warns of living in a rose-tinted past at the present's expense. It can also mark the healthy release of old ties and childhood patterns.
love: In love, it evokes innocent affection, a past lover resurfacing, or reconnecting with why you fell for each other.
career: In career, it can signal returning to earlier passions or working with something from your past.
mood: The mood is nostalgic and sweet — memory softening the present.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is inner-child healing: revisiting innocence to reclaim the simple kindness the soul remembers.

name: Seven of Cups
keywords_upright: choices; fantasy; illusion; wishful thinking; options
keywords_reversed: clarity; decision made; cutting through illusion; overwhelm ending; realism
meaning_upright: The Seven of Cups is a cloud of glittering options, some real and some mirage. It warns against choosing by fantasy alone — examine what each cup actually contains before reaching.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the fog lifts: illusions dissolve, priorities sharpen, and a real choice gets made. Commitment replaces daydreaming.
love: In love, it cautions against idealizing someone or keeping options endlessly open instead of choosing.
career: In career, it signals many possibilities but a need for realistic evaluation — pick one and commit.
mood: The mood is dreamy and indecisive — many doors, all glittering.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is discernment: asking which vision feeds the soul and which merely fascinates it.

name: Eight of Cups
keywords_upright: walking away; seeking deeper meaning; leaving behind; disillusionment; quest
keywords_reversed: fear of leaving; returning; avoidance; staying too long; unfinished departure
meaning_upright: The Eight of Cups is the quiet departure: leaving behind something built because it no longer feeds the soul. It honors the courage to seek deeper meaning even at real cost.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it shows the departure stalled — knowing it's time to go but staying, or drifting away without truly deciding. It can also mark a return to give things one more chance.
love: In love, it signals emotionally outgrowing a situation and the difficult choice to walk away or recommit fully.
career: In career, it points to leaving a stable but unfulfilling path in search of meaningful work.
mood: The mood is wistful and restless — the quiet ache of outgrowing something.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is the soul's quest: leaving the comfortable shore because meaning has moved elsewhere.

name: Nine of Cups
keywords_upright: wishes fulfilled; contentment; satisfaction; pleasure; gratitude
keywords_reversed: superficial happiness; greed; unfulfilled wishes; smugness; inner lack
meaning_upright: The Nine of Cups is the wish card: satisfaction, comfort, and desires coming to fruition. It invites genuine enjoyment and gratitude for what has been achieved.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it questions the satisfaction on display — pleasure that doesn't nourish, smugness, or wishes granted that weren't the right wishes. Fulfillment needs realigning with what actually matters.
love: In love, it signals emotional contentment and wishes in matters of the heart coming true.
career: In career, it marks well-earned satisfaction and rewards — enjoy the achievement without coasting.
mood: The mood is content and satisfied — wishes landing, comfort earned.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is gratitude's test: enjoying fulfillment while asking what truly nourishes beneath the pleasure.

name: Ten of Cups
keywords_upright: lasting happiness; family harmony; emotional fulfillment; love realized; belonging
keywords_reversed: family discord; broken harmony; unrealistic ideals; disconnection at home; strained bonds
meaning_upright: The Ten of Cups is the rainbow over the family: lasting emotional fulfillment, harmony at home, and love realized in daily life. It represents the happy ending that is also a way of living.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it shows the picture and the reality diverging — tension behind the family portrait or ideals straining real relationships. Reconnection asks for honesty over performance.
love: In love, it is a card of deep commitment, family, and shared happiness — the full emotional arc.
career: In career, it signals work-life harmony and an environment that feels like belonging.
mood: The mood is loving and fulfilled — harmony that feels like home.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is love realized: everyday life itself becoming the blessing that was once only wished for.

name: Page of Cups
keywords_upright: emotional openness; creative message; intuition budding; sensitivity; sweet surprise
keywords_reversed: emotional immaturity; moodiness; blocked creativity; escapism; oversensitivity
meaning_upright: The Page of Cups is the tender dreamer bearing an unexpected gift — a sweet message, a creative impulse, or a first stirring of feeling. It encourages openness to whimsy and intuition.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the Page turns moody or escapist: feelings expressed sideways, creativity blocked, or sensitivity tipping into sulking. The emotions are valid; the expression needs maturing.
love: In love, it brings sweet gestures, budding feelings, or a sensitive admirer.
career: In career, it signals creative beginnings and intuitive nudges worth taking seriously.
mood: The mood is playful and sensitive — imagination bubbling up unannounced.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is budding intuition: whimsical messages from the heart that deserve to be taken seriously.

name: Knight of Cups
keywords_upright: romance; charm; invitation; following the heart; idealism
keywords_reversed: unrealistic promises; moodiness; disillusionment; flattery; heart over head gone wrong
meaning_upright: The Knight of Cups rides in with an offer of the heart — romance, an invitation, or an ideal pursued with genuine feeling. It favors following the heart while keeping one foot on the ground.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the Knight's charm outpaces his substance: beautiful words without follow-through, or idealism curdling into disappointment. Judge by actions, not poetry.
love: In love, he is the classic romantic — courtship, proposals, and grand feeling — though reversed his promises need testing.
career: In career, he brings creative proposals and appealing offers; verify the practical details.
mood: The mood is romantic and idealistic — led by the heart, gladly.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is the heart's calling: following feeling as a compass while keeping one foot on the earth.

name: Queen of Cups
keywords_upright: compassion; emotional depth; intuition; nurturing presence; empathy
keywords_reversed: emotional overwhelm; codependency; martyrdom; boundaries dissolved; manipulation through feeling
meaning_upright: The Queen of Cups is deep water held in a graceful vessel: profound empathy, intuition, and emotional wisdom. She listens beneath words and nurtures without drowning.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the Queen loses her shoreline — absorbing everyone's feelings, giving past empty, or using emotion to steer others. Boundaries restore her gift.
love: In love, she embodies emotional attunement and devoted care — a bond where feelings are truly heard.
career: In career, she favors empathic leadership, counseling, and work guided by emotional intelligence.
mood: The mood is empathic and calm — deep waters, gently held.
spiritual: Spiritually, she is compassion embodied: emotional depth as a gift that heals others without drowning the self.

name: King of Cups
keywords_upright: emotional mastery; calm authority; diplomacy; balance of heart and head; wise counsel
keywords_reversed: repressed emotion; coldness; manipulation; moodiness in power; emotional volatility
meaning_upright: The King of Cups sails turbulent seas without capsizing: emotional mastery, diplomacy, and compassion held in steady balance. He advises leading with calm heart rather than reaction.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the King's calm is a lid: repressed feelings leaking as coldness, moodiness, or quiet manipulation. Mastery means feeling fully, not feeling less.
love: In love, he is the emotionally steady partner — supportive and composed, provided the depths are shared, not sealed.
career: In career, he signals wise counsel and diplomacy — staying gracious and level under pressure.
mood: The mood is steady and gracious — feelings present, never in charge.
spiritual: Spiritually, he is mastery of the heart: wisdom that comes from feeling everything and being ruled by none of it.

name: Ace of Swords
keywords_upright: clarity; breakthrough; truth; new idea; mental sharpness
keywords_reversed: confusion; clouded judgment; misused intellect; harsh words; false clarity
meaning_upright: The Ace of Swords is a breakthrough of clarity: the truth cutting cleanly through confusion, a decisive new idea, or a moment of piercing insight. It favors honest speech and clear-eyed decisions.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the blade is dulled or misaimed — muddled thinking, arguments that wound without resolving, or a 'truth' built on faulty premises. Sharpen the facts before swinging.
love: In love, it calls for honest conversation — clarity about feelings and intentions, even when it's uncomfortable.
career: In career, it marks a breakthrough idea or decisive clarity about direction — act on the insight.
mood: The mood is sharp and alert — the mind cutting cleanly through fog.
spiritual: Spiritually, the Ace of Swords is revelation: truth arriving like light, asking to be spoken with care.

name: Two of Swords
keywords_upright: stalemate; difficult choice; avoidance; blocked feelings; truce
keywords_reversed: decision forced; information revealed; stalemate breaking; anxiety; release of denial
meaning_upright: The Two of Swords is a self-imposed stalemate: blindfolded, arms crossed, refusing to look at the choice that must be made. The balance feels safe but costs energy to maintain — deciding is the relief.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the blindfold slips: the deadlock breaks, information surfaces, or the choice is forced by events. Delayed decisions now demand attention.
love: In love, it points to avoidance of a hard conversation or a heart guarded behind crossed swords.
career: In career, it signals a decision being postponed — gather the missing facts and choose.
mood: The mood is torn and guarded — a standstill held with effort.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is stillness before decision: closing the eyes to the noise so inner counsel can be heard.

name: Three of Swords
keywords_upright: heartbreak; painful truth; grief; sorrow; separation
keywords_reversed: healing heartbreak; forgiveness; releasing pain; recovery; old wounds resurfacing
meaning_upright: The Three of Swords is heartbreak rendered honestly: a painful truth, betrayal, or loss that pierces cleanly. Its mercy is precision — this pain names exactly what needs healing.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it marks the slow removal of the swords: forgiveness, healing, and pain beginning to release — though sometimes it flags an old wound reopening to finally heal properly.
love: In love, it signals heartbreak, hard truths, or separation — grief that must be felt before it can pass.
career: In career, it points to a painful disappointment or harsh feedback that, once absorbed, clarifies the path.
mood: The mood is hurt and raw — a truth that stings.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is heartbreak as opening: pain that names exactly what needs healing, so healing can begin.

name: Four of Swords
keywords_upright: rest; recovery; retreat; stillness; recharging
keywords_reversed: burnout; restlessness; forced rest; returning to action; exhaustion ignored
meaning_upright: The Four of Swords is sanctioned rest: a deliberate retreat to recover before the next battle. It counsels stillness, sleep, and mental quiet as strategy, not weakness.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it warns that rest refused becomes rest imposed — burnout, illness, or collapse. Alternatively it signals recovery complete and a rested return to action.
love: In love, it suggests a pause to breathe and reflect rather than pushing the relationship's questions right now.
career: In career, it calls for genuine downtime and recovery before performance suffers.
mood: The mood is quiet and tired — the system asking for stillness.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is rest as sacred practice: retreat, meditation, and silence as the soul's repair.

name: Five of Swords
keywords_upright: hollow victory; conflict; winning at all costs; discord; self-interest
keywords_reversed: reconciliation; making amends; releasing resentment; lingering grudges; lesson learned
meaning_upright: The Five of Swords is the victory that costs more than it wins: the argument won, the relationship damaged. It asks whether being right is worth what it's taking.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it opens the door to reconciliation — apologies, dropped grudges, and the choice to repair over re-fighting. Old resentments may still need conscious release.
love: In love, it warns of fights where winning the point loses the person — pride is the real opponent.
career: In career, it flags cutthroat dynamics or a win that burns bridges; weigh the long-term cost.
mood: The mood is bitter and conflicted — a win that doesn't feel like one.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is choosing peace over victory: releasing the need to be right in favor of what is whole.

name: Six of Swords
keywords_upright: transition; moving on; calmer waters; passage; gradual healing
keywords_reversed: resisting transition; unfinished business; rough crossing; returning to trouble; stuck baggage
meaning_upright: The Six of Swords is the quiet ferry to calmer waters: leaving difficulty behind and moving, gradually, toward relief. The swords come along — lessons carried, not forgotten — but the water ahead is smoother.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the crossing stalls: baggage too heavy to load, a return to the troubled shore, or transition resisted out of fear. The move is still right; the leaving is the hard part.
love: In love, it signals moving past a rough patch together, or transitioning out of a relationship toward peace.
career: In career, it marks a transition — new role, new environment — that trades turbulence for steadier progress.
mood: The mood is subdued but relieved — moving away from rough water.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is passage: carrying the lessons while letting the shore of suffering recede.

name: Seven of Swords
keywords_upright: deception; strategy; acting alone; getting away with it; cunning
keywords_reversed: confession; conscience; exposure; coming clean; self-deception revealed
meaning_upright: The Seven of Swords is the thief tiptoeing from camp: deception, shortcuts, or a strategy that avoids direct confrontation. Sometimes cleverness is warranted — but something here is not being done in the open.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the sneaking ends: secrets surface, conscience speaks, or the deceiver — sometimes oneself — is caught. Coming clean costs less than being found out.
love: In love, it warns of hidden agendas, secrets, or avoidance of honest confrontation.
career: In career, it flags office politics, cut corners, or ideas taken without credit — protect your work and play straight.
mood: The mood is wary and calculating — something not quite in the open.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is honesty with self: noticing where cleverness hides avoidance and choosing the direct path.

name: Eight of Swords
keywords_upright: feeling trapped; self-restriction; victim mindset; mental prison; paralysis
keywords_reversed: liberation; new perspective; self-empowerment; blindfold removed; taking responsibility
meaning_upright: The Eight of Swords is a prison of thought: bound and blindfolded among swords, yet the bindings are loose and the path is clear. The limits are real in feeling but not in fact — the mind built this cage.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the blindfold comes off: recognizing one's own role in the trap and stepping free of it. Perspective returns and options reappear.
love: In love, it points to feeling stuck in a dynamic that beliefs, not circumstances, are maintaining.
career: In career, it signals feeling trapped in a role — the constraints deserve testing, most are assumptions.
mood: The mood is anxious and stuck — trapped by thoughts more than facts.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is freeing the mind: seeing that most cages are beliefs, and beliefs can be set down.

name: Nine of Swords
keywords_upright: anxiety; sleepless nights; worry; dread; mental anguish
keywords_reversed: easing anxiety; facing fears; seeking help; perspective returning; recovery from despair
meaning_upright: The Nine of Swords is the 3 a.m. card: worry magnified in darkness until it fills the room. The anguish is real, but the swords hang on the wall, not in the flesh — fear is outrunning fact.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the night begins to end: fears voiced lose their size, help is accepted, and perspective returns with daylight. The worst imagined rarely arrives.
love: In love, it reflects anxiety and worst-case spirals about the relationship — check fears against reality, ideally out loud.
career: In career, it signals stress and dread outpacing the actual situation; name the worry and it shrinks.
mood: The mood is distressed and sleepless — worry loud in the dark.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is meeting fear with compassion: naming the dread aloud so daylight can right-size it.

name: Ten of Swords
keywords_upright: painful ending; rock bottom; betrayal; collapse; the worst is over
keywords_reversed: recovery; rising again; surviving the ending; old wounds closing; refusing the final lesson
meaning_upright: The Ten of Swords is the definitive ending — dramatic, painful, and complete. Its hidden gift is on the horizon: the sun is rising, and from rock bottom the only road is up.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it shows survival and slow rising after collapse — or a refusal to let something already dead finally end. Recovery begins with accepting that it's over.
love: In love, it marks a painful but final ending or betrayal; the closure, however harsh, frees what comes next.
career: In career, it signals a project or role collapsing completely — salvage the lesson and rebuild elsewhere.
mood: The mood is defeated and drained — an ending felt completely.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is surrender and rebirth: lying down with what is over so the sunrise can begin something new.

name: Page of Swords
keywords_upright: curiosity; vigilance; new ideas; mental energy; truth-seeking
keywords_reversed: gossip; hasty words; scattered thinking; spying; all talk
meaning_upright: The Page of Swords is restless intellect on watch: curious, quick, asking sharp questions and guarding against deception. It favors learning, verifying, and speaking truth even when the voice shakes.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the Page's sharpness cuts sideways — gossip, snooping, hasty conclusions, or big talk without study behind it. Curiosity needs discipline to become insight.
love: In love, it brings probing questions and honest talk, though reversed it warns of suspicion or gossip corroding trust.
career: In career, it signals research, due diligence, and fresh ideas — verify before broadcasting.
mood: The mood is inquisitive and alert — questions flying, eyes open.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is truth-seeking: the watchful mind as a young guardian of honesty.

name: Knight of Swords
keywords_upright: decisive action; ambition; directness; charging ahead; intellectual drive
keywords_reversed: recklessness; aggression; rushing past details; burnout sprint; verbal attacks
meaning_upright: The Knight of Swords charges at full gallop toward the goal — fast, focused, and utterly direct. It rewards decisive action and sharp argument, provided the destination was chosen before the charge.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, speed becomes recklessness: bulldozed feelings, skipped details, and words used as weapons. Slow down enough to see what the charge is trampling.
love: In love, it brings intense, fast-moving pursuit — exciting but potentially inconsiderate of a partner's pace.
career: In career, it favors bold, rapid execution of a clear plan; guard against steamrolling colleagues.
mood: The mood is intense and hasty — thought turned to charge.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is focus tempered: aiming the mind's speed so conviction doesn't outrun wisdom.

name: Queen of Swords
keywords_upright: clear judgment; independence; honest communication; boundaries; experienced wisdom
keywords_reversed: coldness; bitterness; harsh criticism; isolation; cynicism
meaning_upright: The Queen of Swords sees through pretense with compassionate precision: honest, independent, and wise through experience — including painful experience. She counsels clear boundaries and truth delivered cleanly.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, her clarity hardens into cynicism: criticism without warmth, walls without doors. Old pain is doing the talking; the heart deserves a hearing too.
love: In love, she values honesty and independence — genuine connection must respect her boundaries and intelligence.
career: In career, she embodies sharp judgment and fair, direct leadership — say the true thing kindly.
mood: The mood is clear and composed — no illusions, no malice.
spiritual: Spiritually, she is discernment: truth delivered with compassion, boundaries held with grace.

name: King of Swords
keywords_upright: intellectual authority; truth; impartial judgment; strategy; ethical clarity
keywords_reversed: abuse of intellect; cold ruthlessness; manipulation; rigid logic; unjust judgment
meaning_upright: The King of Swords rules by clear thought and fair law: impartial, articulate, and strategically brilliant. He advises deciding from principle and evidence rather than emotion or favoritism.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, intellect turns tyrant — logic weaponized, rules bent to serve power, judgment without mercy. Authority regains legitimacy when truth serves people, not ego.
love: In love, he brings rational steadiness that must remember to speak the heart's language too.
career: In career, he signals authoritative expertise, legal or strategic matters, and decisions that must be scrupulously fair.
mood: The mood is rational and authoritative — thought ordered, judgment calm.
spiritual: Spiritually, he is the ethical mind: clarity in service of fairness, intellect answering to principle.

name: Ace of Pentacles
keywords_upright: new opportunity; prosperity; manifestation; solid start; material gift
keywords_reversed: missed opportunity; shaky foundation; scarcity thinking; delayed reward; poor investment
meaning_upright: The Ace of Pentacles is a tangible new opportunity — a seed of prosperity offered from a steady hand. It favors practical beginnings: the job, the investment, the first brick of something lasting.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the seed slips away or lands on poor soil: an opportunity missed, delayed, or built on shaky ground. Reassess the fundamentals before planting again.
love: In love, it signals a relationship with real-world staying power — grounded, stable, and worth investing in.
career: In career, it marks a concrete opportunity — new job, raise, or venture — with solid long-term potential.
mood: The mood is hopeful and grounded — opportunity that feels solid in the hand.
spiritual: Spiritually, the Ace of Pentacles is the seed of abundance: matter as a place where spirit takes root.

name: Two of Pentacles
keywords_upright: juggling priorities; adaptability; balance in motion; flexibility; managing change
keywords_reversed: overcommitment; dropped balls; disorganization; financial strain; overwhelm
meaning_upright: The Two of Pentacles juggles competing demands with a dancer's rhythm — work and home, income and expense, this and that. It suggests balance is possible, but as ongoing motion rather than a fixed state.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the juggle fails: too many commitments, dropped responsibilities, or finances wobbling. Something must be set down deliberately before it falls.
love: In love, it points to balancing the relationship against life's other demands — presence needs scheduling too.
career: In career, it signals multitasking and shifting priorities; adaptability works until it becomes overload.
mood: The mood is busy and adaptable — juggling kept just barely graceful.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is balance as practice: finding rhythm within motion instead of waiting for stillness.

name: Three of Pentacles
keywords_upright: teamwork; craftsmanship; skill recognized; collaboration; building together
keywords_reversed: poor teamwork; mediocrity; lack of recognition; misaligned goals; working alone
meaning_upright: The Three of Pentacles is mastery meeting collaboration: skilled work recognized and improved by working with others. It marks the stage where talent, planning, and teamwork build something none could alone.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the collaboration frays — credit disputed, standards slipping, or everyone building a different cathedral. Realign on the shared blueprint.
love: In love, it frames the relationship as a joint project — effort, communication, and shared building.
career: In career, it signals successful teamwork and skill being noticed by people who matter.
mood: The mood is cooperative and engaged — skill meeting appreciation.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is craft as devotion: building carefully with others as a quiet form of worship.

name: Four of Pentacles
keywords_upright: security; holding on; control; saving; possessiveness
keywords_reversed: letting go; generosity; loosening control; financial release; spending
meaning_upright: The Four of Pentacles clutches what it has: security achieved, then gripped so tightly nothing new can be received. It affirms prudence while asking what the holding is costing.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the grip opens — generosity, wise spending, or releasing control that was strangling growth. It can also warn of the opposite excess: letting security scatter carelessly.
love: In love, it warns of possessiveness or emotional withholding — safety that comes from control isn't intimacy.
career: In career, it signals financial caution and consolidation, with a caution against hoarding resources or authority.
mood: The mood is cautious and guarded — holding on a little too tightly.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is loosening attachment: learning that security gripped becomes a cage, and released becomes trust.

name: Five of Pentacles
keywords_upright: hardship; scarcity; feeling left out in the cold; financial loss; isolation
keywords_reversed: recovery; help accepted; hardship ending; finding shelter; renewed hope
meaning_upright: The Five of Pentacles is the cold outside the lit window: material hardship, exclusion, or the feeling that help is for other people. The church door is unlocked — support exists, but pride or despair keeps one walking past it.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the hard season turns: help is accepted, finances stabilize, and the sense of exile eases. Recovery accelerates once asking for support stops feeling like failure.
love: In love, it reflects feeling shut out or weathering hardship as a couple — let the other person in from the cold.
career: In career, it signals financial strain or job insecurity; seek the available support rather than enduring alone.
mood: The mood is discouraged and cold — outside the lit window, for now.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is faith through hardship: remembering the door is unlocked and letting help in is not failure.

name: Six of Pentacles
keywords_upright: generosity; giving and receiving; support; fairness; charity
keywords_reversed: strings attached; power imbalance; one-sided giving; debt; self-neglect in giving
meaning_upright: The Six of Pentacles is the flow of generosity in balance: giving from abundance, receiving with grace, and the scales held fair. It signals help arriving — or the means and call to provide it.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the gift has hooks: charity with strings, loans that become leverage, or giving that quietly buys control. Check which side of the scale you're on and what the exchange really costs.
love: In love, it asks whether giving and receiving are balanced — care should circulate, not flow one way.
career: In career, it points to mentorship, raises, or support networks — generosity now returns multiplied.
mood: The mood is generous and balanced — giving and receiving in easy rhythm.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is the sacred flow: abundance circulating, blessing the giver and receiver alike.

name: Seven of Pentacles
keywords_upright: patience; assessment; long-term view; slow growth; waiting for harvest
keywords_reversed: impatience; wasted effort; poor returns; giving up early; misdirected work
meaning_upright: The Seven of Pentacles leans on the hoe and evaluates the vines: effort invested, growth visible, harvest not yet in. It counsels patience and honest assessment — is this crop worth the continued tending?
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it questions the investment: effort poured into thin soil, impatience uprooting what needed time, or persistence in a field that will never yield. Redirect the labor where growth is real.
love: In love, it marks a pause to assess the relationship's growth — investment is judged by trajectory, not just time served.
career: In career, it signals long-term projects maturing slowly; evaluate returns honestly before investing further.
mood: The mood is patient and evaluative — leaning on the hoe, taking stock.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is trust in slow growth: honoring seasons that ripen quietly beneath the surface.

name: Eight of Pentacles
keywords_upright: diligence; skill-building; craftsmanship; dedication; mastery through repetition
keywords_reversed: perfectionism; tedium; cut corners; misplaced effort; burnout from grind
meaning_upright: The Eight of Pentacles is the apprentice at the bench, carving pentacle after pentacle a little better each time. It honors focused practice and the unglamorous repetition that becomes mastery.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the work sours: perfectionism that never ships, corners cut from boredom, or diligence spent on skills that lead nowhere wanted. Reconnect the daily grind to its purpose.
love: In love, it frames connection as craft — small consistent efforts, done attentively, build the bond.
career: In career, it is the card of skill development and dedicated work — the mastery being built now compounds.
mood: The mood is diligent and absorbed — good work, done with care.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is work as meditation: mastery built one mindful repetition at a time.

name: Nine of Pentacles
keywords_upright: self-sufficiency; earned luxury; independence; refinement; enjoying success
keywords_reversed: overdependence; hollow success; overwork without enjoyment; financial insecurity; self-worth tied to wealth
meaning_upright: The Nine of Pentacles stands in a garden of her own making: independence, refinement, and abundance earned by discipline. It invites enjoying the fruits without apology — this was built, not given.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the garden loses its pleasure: success that feels hollow, dependence dressed as luxury, or working so hard for the vineyard there's no time to taste the grapes.
love: In love, it values wholeness over neediness — a partnership of two complete people, or contentment in one's own company.
career: In career, it signals financial independence and well-earned rewards; your work stands on its own merit.
mood: The mood is self-assured and serene — enjoying a garden of one's own making.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is gratitude for earned abundance: independence held lightly, savored fully.

name: Ten of Pentacles
keywords_upright: legacy; lasting wealth; family security; inheritance; long-term foundations
keywords_reversed: family disputes over resources; unstable foundations; short-term thinking; lost inheritance; wealth without belonging
meaning_upright: The Ten of Pentacles is wealth that outlives its maker: family security, legacy, and structures built to last generations. It marks long-term stability and the deep comfort of belonging to something enduring.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the estate shakes: family friction over money, foundations less solid than they look, or riches that never became a home. Rebuild for permanence, not appearance.
love: In love, it points to commitment with deep roots — family, shared assets, a future measured in decades.
career: In career, it signals lasting success, established institutions, and decisions best made on a generational timescale.
mood: The mood is secure and proud — roots deep, table full.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is legacy: blessings tended so well they outlive the hands that planted them.

name: Page of Pentacles
keywords_upright: studiousness; new practical skill; opportunity to learn; grounded ambition; diligent beginnings
keywords_reversed: procrastination; lack of progress; daydreaming without doing; short-lived focus; missed lessons
meaning_upright: The Page of Pentacles studies the coin like a scholar with a fascinating text: eager to learn, practical, and quietly ambitious. It favors starting the course, the apprenticeship, the savings plan — small diligent beginnings.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the studying stalls: plans admired but not started, focus that evaporates, lessons available but unlearned. Ground the dream with one concrete first step.
love: In love, it brings sincere, steady interest that builds slowly and values reliability over fireworks.
career: In career, it signals learning opportunities, new practical ventures, and ambitions pursued step by step.
mood: The mood is studious and motivated — a plan taking earnest shape.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is grounded manifestation: treating study and small steps as the honest magic they are.

name: Knight of Pentacles
keywords_upright: reliability; methodical progress; hard work; patience; commitment to routine
keywords_reversed: stagnation; boredom; stubbornness; workaholism; stuck in a rut
meaning_upright: The Knight of Pentacles is the slowest knight and the only one who always arrives: methodical, reliable, and immune to shortcuts. He counsels steady routine and patient effort toward a distant but certain goal.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, steadiness becomes stagnation: routine hardened into rut, diligence into drudgery, patience into refusal to adapt. The path needs freshening, not abandoning.
love: In love, he is the dependable partner who shows up every time — devotion expressed through consistency rather than drama.
career: In career, he signals slow, thorough progress and trustworthy execution; the tortoise wins this one.
mood: The mood is methodical and steady — unhurried, unstoppable.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is devotion through consistency: showing up daily as its own quiet vow.

name: Queen of Pentacles
keywords_upright: nurturing practicality; resourcefulness; warmth and security; working provider; down-to-earth care
keywords_reversed: self-neglect; work-home imbalance; smothering through providing; financial codependence; depleted caregiver
meaning_upright: The Queen of Pentacles keeps a warm, well-run world: nurturing expressed through practical care — the meal cooked, the budget balanced, the garden thriving. She embodies grounded generosity that makes others feel secure.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the provider runs dry: caring for everyone but herself, juggling work and home past sustainability, or measuring love in provisions. Her garden needs tending too.
love: In love, she offers steady, tangible devotion — a home built together, care shown in daily acts.
career: In career, she signals resourceful management and creating environments where people and projects flourish.
mood: The mood is nurturing and practical — warmth expressed in tangible care.
spiritual: Spiritually, she is the sacred ordinary: body, home, and hearth tended as holy ground.

name: King of Pentacles
keywords_upright: material mastery; abundance; steady leadership; business acumen; reliable provider
keywords_reversed: greed; materialism; rigid control of resources; status obsession; corruption of comfort
meaning_upright: The King of Pentacles rules a prosperous, well-tended realm: business mastery, patient wealth-building, and generosity from genuine abundance. He advises steady stewardship — grow what lasts and share the surplus.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the golden touch corrupts: wealth as scoreboard, control of resources as power over people, comfort defended at any cost. Riches serve life, not the reverse.
love: In love, he is the committed provider whose loyalty is bedrock — warmth must flow alongside the security he builds.
career: In career, he marks the summit of material success — sound investments, business leadership, and enduring prosperity.
mood: The mood is assured and generous — abundance worn comfortably.
spiritual: Spiritually, he is stewardship: wealth as responsibility, prosperity shared as gratitude made visible.
